name: Singleton
def: Ensures a class has exactly one instance and a global access point to it.
problem: Coordinate a single shared resource (config, registry, connection pool) through one instance.
mechanism: A private constructor plus a static accessor lazily creates and caches the lone instance; thread-safe variants guard creation.
whenNot: When you can inject one instance via DI (almost always preferable)
myths: "Singleton guarantees one instance" — per classloader/process only; and it is widely an anti-pattern

antiPatterns: Using it as a global variable for convenience
x.scale: Justified for genuinely single resources; prefer DI-scoped lifetimes otherwise.
